name: Select core
summary: Implement the first downstream Rust command: pairtools-compatible select for exact pair_type predicates.
goal: Implement oracle-tested `pairs-rs select '(pair_type == "UU")'` with normal header/body streaming and loud failures for unsupported select features.
explicit_non_goals: Do not implement merge, dedup, split, stats, restrict, phase, sample, scaling, or filterbycov behavior.
Do not implement a general Python expression engine.
Do not change parse or sort behavior.
Do not benchmark or claim speedup.
acceptance_criteria: `pairs-rs select '(pair_type == "UU")'` matches pairtools oracle output on committed small pairs and pairsam fixtures.
`-o/--output` writes selected rows to a file and stdout is used when output is omitted.
Unsupported select predicates and unsupported select options fail loudly with `not implemented`.
known_risks: Pairtools select supports a broad expression language; M110 intentionally starts with exact pair_type equality only.
